entities.name: MICRONUTRIENT DEFICIENCY
entities.description: A significant public health problem in Ethiopia characterized by a lack of essential vitamins and minerals

entities.name: ETHIOPIA
entities.description: The country where the study on food fortification and consumer demand was conducted

entities.name: FOOD FORTIFICATION
entities.description: A cost-effective and sustainable strategy to deliver essential micronutrients to a population

entities.name: SAFETY CERTIFICATION
entities.description: A certification issued by government or private parties to nudge consumers toward choosing fortified foods

entities.name: NUTRITION EDUCATION MESSAGES
entities.description: Information provided to consumers about the benefits of micronutrients to influence their food choices

entities.name: COOKING OIL
entities.description: A food product identified as an ideal candidate for vitamin A fortification in Ethiopia

entities.name: VITAMIN A FORTIFICATION
entities.description: The specific process of adding vitamin A to cooking oil to address nutrient deficiencies

entities.name: WILLINGNESS TO PAY
entities.description: The maximum price consumers are willing to pay for safety certification and vitamin A fortification

entities.name: STATED CHOICE EXPERIMENT
entities.description: A research method used to reveal consumer preferences and calculate willingness to pay

entities.name: URBAN CONSUMERS
entities.description: The target human population in Ethiopia used to study preferences for fortified cooking oil

entities.name: VITAMIN A
entities.description: An essential micronutrient whose fortification in cooking oil was the focus of the study
